note_tweet.text:
Nova Survivor Shirel Golan took her own life on her 22nd bday, just a few days after the anniversary of the October 7 Massacre. 11 of her friends were murdered at Nova, while she survived. 

In the aftermath of WW2, suicide rates among Holocaust survivors were estimated to be up to 25% higher than the rest of the population. 

Many suffered from severe depression and long term PTSD from the horrors they experienced. 

The same severe depression and PTSD has been plaguing the survivors of the horrors of the October 7 Massacre, and their families. 

The survivors need support. They need mental health services, counseling, and therapy. 

The Tribe of Nova Foundation is a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing the mental health services October 7 survivors desperately need. 

Please consider donating in Shirel’s memory. Link below: ⬇️⬇️
https://t.co/5TXJVB247V
